result:
Plan is complete and the summary record has been written to `orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl`.

---

## Summary

**EPAM-008 is a review gate, not a new implementation.** The plan is 14 sequential audit steps across ~15 source files and 2 command executions.

**Biggest risk: test coverage gaps (R1 — High/High).** Inspection of `test/unit/` reveals only 8 existing test files, yet 10 stories added substantial new features. AC#8 requires "all new features have corresponding vitest unit tests" — 6 new test files are likely needed:

| Missing Test File | Covers |
|---|---|
| `test/unit/agent/ProfileLoader.test.ts` | EPAM-004 |
| `test/unit/agent/PlanMode.test.ts` | EPAM-005 |
| `test/unit/decisions/DecisionStore.test.ts` | EPAM-006 |
| `test/unit/mcp/McpServer.test.ts` | EPAM-038 |
| `test/unit/scaffold/ScaffoldRunner.test.ts` | EPAM-037 |
| `test/unit/commands/slashCommands.test.ts` | EPAM-030/031/032 |

**The review agent should determine on step 10–11 whether to flag these as blocking or write them inline.** The PRD's 3.5-minute AI time estimate is conservative — a thorough audit will consume 8–12 minutes of AI time and closer to $0.35–0.45 in cost.
